pages.text: Soul sand seems far more common here than anywhere else. Perhaps that has something to do with the lush and almost impenetrable vegetation that I haven't found elsewhere here. Local fauna often gets caught in the different thorns and brambles; perhaps both plants and soil find nourishment in this deadly trap?
Other than that, there doesn't seem to be many noteworthy elements here, although these plants might serve some purpose, so I make a note of them regardless.

pages.title: Bramble
pages.text: This large and thick bramble is not exclusive to the Nether, but I have never seen it in such quantities elsewhere. If I collect it carefully, it can make for an effective and painful hedge.

pages.title: Thornstalk
pages.text: Of no particular use. A nasty-looking thorn that protrudes directly from the depths of the sands. It sways and rocks slowly, as if there was someone inside.
